## Neural network (small MLP) — API literacy

- **`mlp(hidden_units = …, penalty = …, epochs = …) |> set_engine("nnet")`** — one hidden layer, weight decay, counts passes through the data.
- **Requires scaling** (already in `rec_base` via `step_normalize`).
- On **small tabular** data, trees often match or beat MLPs — still worth knowing the **same workflow** interface.

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-spec
#| echo: true
mlp_spec <- mlp(hidden_units = 8, penalty = 0.1, epochs = 150) |>
  set_engine("nnet", trace = FALSE) |>
  set_mode("classification")

wf_mlp <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rec_base) |>
  add_model(mlp_spec)
```

```{r}
#| label: day04-mlp-resamples
#| echo: true
#| message: false
set.seed(9)
rs_mlp <- fit_resamples(wf_mlp, folds, metrics = metrics_cls)
collect_metrics(rs_mlp) |>
  filter(.metric %in% c("roc_auc", "accuracy")) |>
  select(.metric, mean, std_err)
```

Full three-way comparison: [Module 08](../modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd).
